    pfs="Standard"
>
[Liar's Gun](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1173)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cobbled" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=400" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 157

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Pepperbox](/Weapons.aspx?ID=205)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This dubious weapon gets its name from the fact that it's typically crafted with multiple false barrels so that it's more difficult for enemies to predict the weapon's angle of fire from the four working barrels. Most of the time, this gun functions as a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [pepperbox](/Weapons.aspx?ID=205)_ with [capacity 4](/Traits.aspx?ID=399) instead of capacity 3, albeit one with a complicated loading mechanism involving rotating the barrels. In a pinch though, all four of the real barrels can be fired simultaneously.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)    **Effect** Make up to four Strikes with the _liar's gun_. Each of the four Strikes must be against a different target within a 20-foot cone. You apply and increasing your multiple attack penalty for the four Strikes only after resolving all attacks. If the weapon misfires on any of these attacks, wait to apply the misfire until you resolve all of the attacks

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Liar's Gun

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Arboreal's Revenge](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1174)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 160

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This walnut and brass _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [blunderbuss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=196)_ has a natural look, with wood worn smooth by time, but left knotted and gnarled. The handgrip beneath the flared muzzle is a well-positioned bulging tree knot, and the long stock looks as if it was grown to fit you. On closer examination, the whorls and rings in the wood resemble eyes.

 _Arboreal's revenge_ is a haunted firearm constructed with wood hewn from a living [arboreal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=8), slaying the arboreal and trapping its spirit in the firearm. Perhaps a patient wielder could one day put the arboreal's spirit to rest, or at least come to terms with it, abating the drawbacks of the weapon while keeping its advantages.

 When you first fire _arboreal's revenge_, the blunderbuss fuses with you, after which it's almost impossible to remove it from your possession with a [_remove curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=250) or similar effect, like many cursed items. You gain weakness 5 to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) and the flat check for you to recover from [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) increases from 15 to 17, or from 10 to 12 if you receive appropriate help. 

Whenever you wield _arboreal's revenge_, the arboreal spirit bound to the blunderbuss attempts to overtake you. The wood handgrip sprouts roots which grow around your hand, binding both of your hands to the firearm. You gain a +4 circumstance bonus to your Reflex DC when defending against checks to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) you. However, you can't Release your grip on the blunderbuss until you forcibly tear off the roots, though they do allow you to move your hands enough (and only enough) to reload and fire the blunderbuss. Tearing off the roots takes 1 minute.

 The arboreal haunting the firearm can use the following two effects whenever it wants, often using them at random. You can coax it into performing them each intentionally once per day, with the following activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The arboreal haunting the blunderbuss attempts to regrow itself into a new physical body by sprouting from the bodies of all the creatures that the firearm recently harmed. You and all creatures within 60 feet damaged by arboreal's revenge within the last minute must attempt a DC 20 Fortitude saving throw. On a failure, they take a –10-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds as roots rapidly sprout from their wounds, which lasts until they [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 20).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_barkskin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=20) from the blunderbuss as a 2nd-level primal spell. However, the weakness to fire imposed by barkskin is cumulative with the weakness to fire imposed by this weapon's curse, for a total of weakness 8 to fire.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Arboreal's Revenge

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Immolation Clan Pistol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1175)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 160

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This charred and blackened _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [clan pistol](/Feats.aspx?ID=3281)_ is coated in a thick layer of soot and grease and its retort sounds uncomfortably like a scream. Clearly at dangerous risk of misfire, this weapon claimed the life of its dwarven crafter shortly after completion and is haunted by that pained spirit. This ever-burning spirit keeps the firearm warm to the touch, regardless of surrounding environment.

 No matter how much you clean and maintain it, an _immolation clan pistol_ is always at risk of a misfire, and the flat check to avoid a misfire is DC 7 rather than DC 5. On a misfire, an _immolation clan pistol_ releases a blast of concentrated fire, dealing you 2d6 fire damage and 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

 An _immolation clan pistol_ fuses with you when you first fire it. While you're fused with the _immolation clan pistol_, the pained spirit haunts your dreams, forcing you to relive its death each night. If you fired the _immolation clan pistol_ that day, you are unharmed by these dreams. If you didn't fire the _immolation clan pistol_ that day, but still possess it, you awaken fatigued. If you no longer possess the _immolation clan pistol_, the spirit of its dwarven smith is enraged by your neglect; you awaken fatigued and aflame, taking 2d6 persistent fire damage.

 Regardless, these nightmares possess clues to the spirit's life. If you take the _immolation clan pistol_ to the site of its creation and repair it with tools belonging to the tormented spirit, you might be able to break the curse. If the spirit is set free, this weapon becomes a _+2 greater striking [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [clan pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=193)_ with no other abilities or curses.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You allow yourself to become partially possessed by the immolated spirit bound to the clan pistol. For 1 minute, you look like a flaming corpse. During this time, you gain a +2 status bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks and the immolation clan pistol becomes a _+2 striking flaming clan pistol_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Immolation Clan Pistol

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pact-Bound Pistol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1176)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 161

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Wielded in over a hundred duels by the famed Ustalavic noblewoman Countess Tasya Iserav, this _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [fearsome](/Equipment.aspx?ID=731) [dueling pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=201)_ is a work of master craftsmanship, almost eerie in the axiomatic perfection present in each dimension and detail.

 Commissioned at great expense and artfully designed by a team of the finest gunsmiths, this exquisite mahogany pistol has [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271) detailing. The ruby-eyed, leering [devil's](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=30) face that surrounds the barrel was intended to strike fear into the Countess's opponents—of which there were many. Countess Tasya credited this weapon with her exceptional success in dueling circles and refused to name the weapon's creators for fear their works would fall into an opponent's hands. In the past decade, seven replicas of the Countess's famed pistol have arisen, each said to be crafted by the Countess's anonymous gunsmiths. That each of these weapon's wielders have quickly risen to become exceptional duelists in their own right has lent credence to their claims. Despite public pressure, wielders of these replicas have refused to square off against the Countess.

 Despite the weapon's beauty and utility, a _pact-bound pistol_ is a cursed weapon and a physical manifestation of an infernal contract sworn between the bearer of the weapon and the Archdevil [Mephistopheles](/Deities.aspx?ID=69). Each _pact-bound pistol_ was crafted not by a team of master artisans, but by Countess Tasya Iserav herself, a secret diabolist and devoted priestess of The Crimson Son. The very act of firing the weapon is an acceptance of the contract's terms and causes the weapon to fuse to you. Thereafter, the weapon reappears in your possessions within 1 minute of you discarding it, and can't be destroyed or thrown away permanently, until the curse is removed.

 Whenever you attack a living creature with the _pact-bound pistol_, text from the infernal contract is seared into your flesh, causing you to lose 1 Hit Point, or 2 Hit Points if the attack was a critical success. The text appears one sentence at a time, from the beginning to the end and is written in [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19). When the contract is fully scribed (see below), you no longer lose Hit Points when you attack a living creature. These burned phrases heal over as scars which can't be removed while you are fused to the weapon, although they can be covered and disguised.

 Each time a creature dies within 1 hour of being damaged by a _pact-bound pistol_, that creature must attempt a DC 25 Will saving throw. On a failure, their soul is consigned to the Archdevil Mephistopheles; their soul immediately goes to [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=18) and they can't be returned to life through any means short of divine intervention, a heist, or a successful appeal in the courts of Hell; even powerful magic such as [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377) is insufficient. After you have consigned 9 souls to Mephistopheles in this way, the contract is fully scribed upon your flesh. You become immune to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) damage and the _pact-bound pistol_ becomes a _+2 striking greater fearsome dueling pistol_. Your soul belongs to Mephistopheles and immediately goes to Hell when you die. You can't be returned to life through any means, even through the divine intervention of a deity.

 Failing to wield a _pact-bound pistol_ is a breach of contract. Each day you go without firing the _pact-bound pistol_ at a living creature, you must attempt a Will saving throw. On a failure, you become [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37). If you are already stupefied, the value of your stupefied condition increases by 1, to a maximum of stupefied 4. While you're fused with the _pact-bound pistol_, you can't reduce the value of your stupefied condition except by damaging a living creature with it. Whenever you damage a living creature with it, if you are suffering from the stupefied condition due to the _pact-bound pistol_, your stupefied condition is removed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pact-Bound Pistol

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Animate Dreamer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1177)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 161

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dwarven Scattergun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=207)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +26; precise vision 60 feet, imprecise hearing 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), and six other common languages)

**Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +30, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +27, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +27, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +30

**Int** +6, **Wis** +4, **Cha** +4

**Will** +26 

</column>

---        

The gunsmith that created this marvelous _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [spell-storing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=305) [scattergun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=207)_ poured so much love and care into its creation that the weapon gained a spark of sentience. However, at first it was completely incapable of expressing itself. This led a seething frustration to grow within the weapon, as it yearned desperately to respond to the same love and affection that created it. Through decades of effort, it gained the ability to communicate empathically, then telepathically. Now, the weapon is capable of exerting its influence over other inanimate objects. Despite the weapon's progress, years of feeling helpless have given the _animate dreamer_ a singular goal: to obtain and occupy a body of its own.

 An _animate dreamer_ is cunning, intelligent, and patient. It urges you to create a body for it and is willing to go to any lengths to see its goals come to fruition, including coercion, deception, and violence. An _animate dreamer_ desires a permanent body but isn't picky about the body's form or the methods it has to use in order to gain it. Therefore, an _animate dreamer_ is just as happy in a living body stolen from an innocent as it would be in an artificially constructed body, or even an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) corpse. If you refuse to work towards creating or obtaining a body for the _animate dreamer_, it will likely use its possession ability to try and take control of you and use your body to find a permanent replacement for itself.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)   **Requirements** The _animate dreamer_ has a stored spell. It can see a creature it hit and damaged within the last minute, and that creature's within 120 feet of _animate dreamer_; **Effect** The _animate dreamer_ casts its stored spell at a target that meets the requirements. This empties the spell from the weapon and allows a spell to be cast into it again. The animate dreamer has a spell attack roll of +25 and a spell DC of 33 with the stored spell.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)    **Effect** The _animate dreamer_ focuses on a single unattended inanimate object in an area it can see and exerts its will over the object, temporarily levitating it around. The animate dreamer casts [_mage hand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=177) as a 5th-level occult spell.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _animate dreamer_ attempts to achieve its goal of occupying a body of its own and casts [_possession_](/Spells.aspx?ID=225) as a 7th-level spell with a spell DC of 33. The weapon still functions as a _+2 greater striking spell-storing scattergun_ while this effect is active but loses all other special abilities until the spell expires and the _animate dreamer's_ intellect returns to it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Animate Dreamer

    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Boastful Hunter](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1178)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 162

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 or 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Jezail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=203)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +11; precise vision 30 feet, imprecise hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1))

**Skills** [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +9, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +10, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +11

**Int** +0, **Wis** +2, **Cha** +3

**Will** +11 

</column>

---        

Possessing a boisterous, proud demeanor, this _+1 [jezail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=203)_ is an aspiring big game hunter, always quick to share a tale of its bold adventures and predatory conquests—although the veracity of such tales is often questionable. A _boastful hunter_ enjoys tracking and hunting animals of all kinds but takes particular pleasure in taking down large, dangerous, or rare animals. Against an [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9), the _boastful hunter_ deals 1d6 additional damage. On a critical hit against an animal, the _boastful hunter_ also deals 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

 A _boastful hunter_ urges you to face off against tougher and tougher creatures—a challenge the ambitious rifle may not be adequate to deal with. If the _boastful hunter_ goes one week without participating in a hunt against an animal of at least your level, it becomes bored. A bored _boastful hunter_ complains incessantly and imposes a –1 item penalty to attack rolls against non-animal targets. A bored _boastful hunter_ can be appeased by using it in combat against an animal of at least your level.

 Unable to accept its own weaknesses, a _boastful hunter_ blames any failed hunts on you, and considers remarks about its strength or quality a terrible insult. An insulted _boastful hunter_ badmouths you to sentient creatures you interact with, imposing a –1 item penalty to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), and [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) skill checks, and all attacks with it incur a misfire chance until the weapon is appeased. An insulted _boastful hunter_ can be appeased by complimenting it and succeeding at a Diplomacy check against its Will DC two days in a row. You can only attempt a Diplomacy check to appease the _boastful hunter_ once each day.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Boastful Hunter

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Duchy Defender](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1179)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 162

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Flintlock Musket](/Weapons.aspx?ID=191)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +26; precise vision 60 feet, imprecise hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Kelish](/Languages.aspx?ID=38), [Osiriani](/Languages.aspx?ID=40))

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +23, [Alkenstar Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +25, Firearm Lore +25, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +21

**Int** +2, **Wis** +5, **Cha** +4

**Will** +26 

</column>

---        

Patriotic to the extreme, this _+2 [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [flintlock musket](/Weapons.aspx?ID=191)_ was among the first firearms forged in the Gunworks and was wielded by Ancil Alkenstar, founder of the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar. In Alkenstar's hands, the musket was used to defend the burgeoning Grand Duchy from outside threats, including [mutants](/Traits.aspx?ID=229) of the Mana Wastes, Nexian [constructs](/Traits.aspx?ID=35), and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) from Geb. After Ancil's death, the weapon was passed down, per his own decree, not to his heirs but to the greatest defender of Alkenstar, as determined by the weapon's previous owner. Over time, the devotion and heroism of each successive wielder left a psychic imprint on the weapon, until it developed an intellect and drive of its own.

 A _duchy defender_ is a powerful weapon with a rigid personality. It follows the laws of Alkenstar to the letter, and fights in defense of its nation. If these two priorities ever clash, a _duchy defender_ prioritizes the protection of Alkenstar and its citizens over following the laws themselves, since laws can change, but the people will always need protecting.

  If you break the laws of Alkenstar, a _duchy defender_ goes out of its way to call attention to you at inopportune times, such as when you're engaged in an illegal activity, and attempts to see you pay for your crimes, up to and including outing you to law enforcement and providing testimony against you in court. If a _duchy defender_ is used unjustly against a citizen of Alkenstar, the weapon automatically misfires.

 If the weapon is removed from the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar without a reason that directly benefits the duchy, the _duchy defender_ transports itself away, reappearing in the hands of a previous worthy owner, or elsewhere in Alkenstar if no such previous owner lives. In the hands of a worthy owner, a _duchy defender_ is nearly impossible to steal, since it transports itself back to you at the first opportunity after someone takes it, and it grants you a +2 circumstance bonus to their Reflex DC to resist being disarmed of it.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** The _duchy defender_ focuses its will on a single target it can see and who it knows is guilty of committing a crime within Alkenstar's borders. The next time the _duchy defender_ damages this target, it deals an additional 2d8 precision damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710))    **Effect** The _duchy defender_ travels through the air at a speed faster than light, returning to the hands of a previous owner who has been deemed worthy. If the owner's hands are full, the _duchy defender_ instead appears on the ground in their space. If there is no previous owner the _duchy defender_ deems worthy that lives, it instead travels to a random public location within Alkenstar. Traveling to an unknown location is draining; after doing so, the _duchy defender_ can't transport itself anywhere for 1d4 days.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Duchy Defender

>
[Sunken Pistol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1180)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 163

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dragon-Mouth Pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=200)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +12; precise vision 30 feet, imprecise hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1))

**Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19, [Navigation Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +14, Sailing Lore +14

**Int** +0, **Wis** +2, **Cha** +4

**Will** +16 

</column>

---        

A _sunken pistol_ is imbued with the unfulfilled desires and insatiable greed of its previous wielder, a notorious pirate drowned at sea. Once a beautiful and artistically wrought weapon, a _sunken pistol_ is coated in a fine layer of dried salt and encrusted with barnacles. Each shot fired from this _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [dragon mouth pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=200)_ is now coated in a layer of salty ectoplasm and has the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) rune. You can shoot the _sunken pistol_ underwater, even when using black powder ammunition.

 A _sunken pistol_ urges you to amass a horde of treasure even a dragon would be proud of. The methods used to acquire these riches matter not to the greedy pirate pistol, so long as you amass and hoard excessive wealth. Above all else, a _sunken pistol_ desires the return of its own treasure, which might rest in the wreck of its sunken ship's hold or be hidden on a dangerous island.

 In pursuit of treasure, a sunken pistol offers nautical and navigation advice to you, though it refuses such aid during less lucrative ventures. If you prove a profitable partner, the sunken pistol aids you in combat by [Demoralizing](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) your opponents (see the first Activation below). If you prove charitable or unwilling to retrieve its treasure, the _sunken pistol_ turns its ire on you, Demoralizing you during combat until you relent. If you repeatedly refuse the _sunken pistol_, it would be wise to not accept [_water breathing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=370) from it, as it might attempt to drown you to allow a more suitable wielder to acquire it from your corpse. The _sunken pistol_ can use the following activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** The sunken pistol curses and insults a creature it can see, attempting an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check to Demoralize that creature.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The sunken pistol casts _water breathing_ on you as a 3rd-level arcane spell. The sunken pistol can Dismiss this spell, so be sure to keep the gun happy if you're relying on its good graces to breathe!

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sunken Pistol

>
[Hyldarf's Fang](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1181)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 164

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Hand Cannon](/Weapons.aspx?ID=193)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [hand cannon](/Weapons.aspx?ID=193)_ is carved and crafted from a single large fang, worn with age and cracked with red lines. The tip of the fang has been filed down, but still leaks black fluid occasionally. It was fashioned from the tooth of the [tor linnorm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=287) Hyldarf by a half-Ulfen gunsmith from Tian Xia who sought the title of linnorm king. Though the smith failed to slay the linnorm, he did claim the mighty dragon's tooth and fashion it into a magic firearm that still drips warm venom. Hyldarf survived the encounter with her attacker and slew the gunsmith years later, though by then the smith had already bequeathed the weapon to his chosen heir and it was far out of her grasp. The linnorm still searches for her missing tooth, portending potential doom for the weapon's owner.

 The weapon deals an additional 2d6 fire damage on a successful Strike, plus 3d10 persistent fire damage on a critical hit. Fire damage dealt by this weapon (including [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and damage from Hyldarf's Venom) ignores the target's [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) resistance.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You call upon Hyldarf's power to gain the magic of a linnorm for a brief time. For 1 minute, you gain the effects of [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=125), [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128), and [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) as well as fire resistance 20.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You soak your shot in the fluid of the fang, imbuing it with Hyldarf's venom. The next Strike you make with Hydalf's Fang before the end of your next turn delivers the venom to the target.

 **Hyldarf's Venom** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [injury](/Traits.aspx?ID=97), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 34; **Maximum Duration** 3 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d6 fire damage and [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34); **Stage 2** 6d6 fire damage and sickened 2.

</column>

[Iris of the Sky](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1182)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 165

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2

This _+1 striking [jezail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=203)_ is built from white hot metal and has a ruby fused into the palm wood stock. When the _iris of the sky_ misfires, you take 5 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

 The first firearm of this type came as the result of a gunslinger who entreated an [efreeti](/Monsters.aspx?ID=214), wishing for the might of the desert sun. The weapon constantly glows and burns with a brilliant intensity. The firearm has since belonged to an extensive string of users and been replicated several times, though each wielder of the weapon, whether the original or one of its copies, has eventually ended up as a charred husk, slain by fire in battle or unusual accidents that no one could quite explain except, perhaps, the efreet.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You focus the solar energy housed in the ruby to release a gout of solar flame instead of a bullet. Make a Strike with the _iris of the sky_. All damage from this Strike is fire damage and the target takes an additional 1d6 fire damage and 1d6 persistent fire damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You make a wish into the _iris of the sky_, yearning for it to strike true, and then fire. Strike against a foe with the iris, rolling the attack roll twice and taking the better result. If the attack is a failure, you take 5 persistent fire damage.

</column>

>
[Mountebank's Passage](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1183)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 165

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Flintlock Pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=192)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [flintlock pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=192)_ has the odd construction of possessing two triggers, one of soapstone and one of onyx, clearly separated with individual trigger guards. Originally created by a student at Blythir College in Alkenstar, the _mountebank's passage_ has the ability to create temporary linked portals on existing surfaces. The weapon disappeared shortly after its invention, but rumors have circulated that it now belongs to a group of thieves who use it to commit impossible robberies.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)   **Requirements** the _mountebank's passage_ isn't loaded; **Effect** You pull the soapstone trigger. Choose a vertical surface within 120 feet. A beam of white energy crackles to the vertical surface and creates a white portal on that surface. Any creature who moves through the white portal comes out through the _mountebank's passage's_ black portal, if one exists on the same plane. Using this activation causes any previous white portal to disappear, even if you don't create a new portal; otherwise, the portal lasts until your next daily preparations.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)   **Requirements** the _mountebank's passage_ isn't loaded; **Effect** You pull the onyx trigger. Choose a vertical surface within 120 feet. A beam of black energy crackles to the vertical surface and creates a black portal on that surface. Any creature who moves through the black portal comes out through the _mountebank's passage's_ white portal, if one exists on the same plane. Using this activation causes any previous black portal to disappear; even if you don't create a new portal; otherwise, the portal lasts until your next daily preparations.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mountebank's Passage

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Reaper's Grasp](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1184)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 165

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Arquebus](/Weapons.aspx?ID=195)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+2 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [arquebus](/Weapons.aspx?ID=518)_ has an ashwood stock plated with worked silver, featuring a mosaic of agonized skulls carved into the metal. The mere mention of Galt’s infamous [_final blades_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1094) is enough to make any mortal creature shudder. Used to execute political opponents in the nation’s constant civil war, these guillotines hold the souls of those they execute, preventing them from reaching a natural afterlife. When one of the final blades was destroyed in 4710, some of the metal from its remains made its way to Alkenstar and was reforged into a deadly firearm that retained the guillotine’s soul-stealing properties.

Whenever the _reaper’s grasp_ kills a sapient living or undead creature, a portion of that creature’s soul is drawn inexorably into the weapon, and another skull is added to the mosaic as if scrawled by an invisible hand, to a maximum of 10 souls. Creatures whose souls are protected by outside forces, such as a [lich’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3082) soul cage, are immune to this effect, though the process, while painful, does not prevent the soul from going to the afterlife or being resurrected. Keep track of the level of each creature whose souls are stored in the _reaper’s grasp_

**Activate—Soul Shot** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))   **Requirements** There's at least one soul stored in the _reaper's grasp_; **Effect** You channel the necromantic energy of a soul stored in the weapon into your next attack, increasing the attack's destructive power. Choose a soul stored in the weapon. The next Strike you make with the _reaper's grasp_ before the end of your turn deals an additional negative damage equal to twice the level of the creature whose soul you chose. Using this ability releases the chosen soul, and a skull on the mosaic fades away.

**Activate—Devour Captured** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))   **Requirements** There's at least one soul stored in the _reaper's grasp_; **Effect** You channel the necromantic energy of a soul stored in the weapon into a mantle around you, protecting you from harm. Choose a soul stored in the weapon. You gain temporary Hit Points equal to three times the level of the creature whose soul you chose, which last until the end of your next turn. Using this ability releases the chosen soul, and a skull on the mosaic fades away.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Reaper's Grasp

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rowan Rifle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1185)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 166

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

In a dense forest within Arcadia, [djezet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1410) fell from the sky millennia ago, even before Earthfall. While this metal was poisonous to most of the plant life growing nearby, one stubborn rowan tree refused to die, its roots slowly absorbing djezet in small particles from the ground until the entire plant was suffused with it. [Fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) bowyers discovered the remarkable plant and coaxed it into growing into a very particular shape, its branching trunks woven together into a tightly twisted and naturally rifled barrel.

 One of the legendary star guns, Arcadia's greatest treasures, the _Rowan Rifle_ is a _+2 [greater fearsome](/Equipment.aspx?ID=731) [speed](/Equipment.aspx?ID=304) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280)_ advanced firearm with a [range of increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) of 300 feet. It deals 1d8 fire damage and has the [backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=172), [concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=401), and [fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=178) traits.

 As a star gun, the _Rowan Rifle_ runs on magic, and doesn't use ammunition or black powder. As a weapon forged to protect the fey peoples of Arcadia, the _Rowan Rifle's_ enchantments prevent it from ever causing harm to a creature with the fey trait; any attempt to shoot a fey with it results in an automatic misfire. This legendary star gun is as much a badge of office as a weapon, as it denotes dominion over all Briarbough. If the _Rowan Rifle's_ wielder is fey, or if they were specifically given the _Rowan Rifle_ by a fey creature who recognized them as a worthy champion, they can use the following activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)    **Effect** You will the _Rowan Rifle_ to change the energy it fires from the white-hot energy of summer that glows like a star to the shivering chill of winter, the crackling electricity of the storm, or even the sonic vibrations of a roar. The _Rowan Rifle's_ damage type changes from its current damage type to cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. On the next sunrise, the _Rowan Rifle_ returns to dealing fire damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** The _Rowan Rifle_ fires a 60-foot line of liquid djezet that wraps around all creatures in the affected area before hardening, which impedes them with metallic vines unless the affected creature succeeds at a DC 35 Reflex save. A creature who fails the save takes a –15-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds, and a creature who critically fails is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24). A creature who succeeds at a DC 35 [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) check ends this effect. Otherwise, the djezet vines last for 10 minutes before crumbling away.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;   **Effect** You hold aloft the _Rowan Rifle_ and step into a tree in Briarbough, and you tree stride to another tree in Briarbough.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Rowan Rifle

    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tiger's Claw](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1186)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 166

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dueling Pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=201)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This +2 _[striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [fearsome](/Equipment.aspx?ID=731) [dueling pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=201)_ is made from fine tigerwood, with the head of a tiger as the muzzle. Beneath the tiger head is a claw shaped [bayonet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=186). One of a set of four guns crafted as a gift to a Zenj family for delivery of rare healing and disease-abating herbs during an outbreak of a deadly disease in the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar, these firearms are now passed down to those who have done brave acts in service to the Zenj people. The flintlock sparks thrown by this weapon take the shape of pouncing tigers and the firearm's report sounds like a tiger's growl. Clever wielders use the firearm's report to panic their prey into mistakes and then pounce for the kill.

 This firearm's bullets deal slashing damage instead of piercing and add an additional 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) on a critical hit. This persistent bleed damage causes tiger-claw-shaped wounds to appear on the target.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You capitalize on the fears your firearm engenders, terrifying your foes. Make a ranged Strike with this firearm against a target. If you successfully deal damage to your target, the target is also affected by a 4th-level [_phantasmal killer_](/Spells.aspx?ID=219) with a spell DC of 28. While _phantasmal killer_ typically takes the shape of the target's worst fear, this effect always appears to the target in the form of a majestic and ferocious tiger.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tiger's Claw

markdown:
**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 167 **Price** 3,000 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Base Weapon** [Flintlock Pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=192) --- This _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [flintlock pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=192)_ bears strange, jagged markings of erratic design and has an oddly squishy grip. It can be activated to produce a variety of unusual effects. **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Effect** Choose a creature within 60 feet and roll a percentile die on the table below to determine the pistol's effect. If an entry lists a spell, the pistol Casts that Spell at the indicated level (or at its lowest level, if no level is listed). You make any decisions for a spell cast by the pistol unless otherwise indicated, except that it must only target the creature you chose, or the creature you chose must be the center of the spell's area, if it has an area but no targets. The only exception to the limitation on targeting is if the effect specifically states it targets you. If the spell's range is less than 60 feet, increase the range to 60 feet. Any spell DC required is DC 29, and any spell attack roll required is +21. If the pistol casts a spell on you, you don't get a saving throw or any other defense against it. Once activated, the pistol can't be activated again for 1d4 hours. ## Pistol of Wonder
**d%****Wondrous Effect**
1–2The pistol casts [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1694) (3rd rank) to produce a hippogriff adjacent to you; only you can ride it.
3The target becomes a shiny metal color (bronze, copper, or iron) and any sound or speech becomes tinny and robotic in nature; this effect lasts 1 month.
4–5The pistol casts [_planar tether_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1636).
6[_Rewrite Memory_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655) (6th level) causes the target to forget you ever existed; you can't Sustain the Spell.
7–13A short rod protrudes from the pistol and unfurls a small flag that reads “Bang!”. The pistol can't be fired until the flag is removed, which requires a single Interact action.
14–16You are [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 30 feet directly away from the target; if you hit an object or creature, you stop, but take falling damage equal to the distance you moved (if you hit a creature, it takes the same amount of falling damage).
17–19The target is pushed 30 feet directly away from you; if it hits an object or creature, it stops, but takes falling damage equal to the distance it moved (if it hits another creature, the other creature takes the same amount of falling damage).
20You don't need to eat or drink for 1 week.
21–25The pistol casts [_toxic cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722).
26–30The pistol casts [_heroism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=149) on you.
31The pistol casts [_one with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) on you, except you appear as a Medium wooden grave marker bearing your name and the current date; you can't Dismiss the spell for 1 round.
32The pistol casts [_mask of terror_](/Spells.aspx?ID=185), except the target appears as a more fearsome and violent version of itself to all observers.
33–35The target knows you have a bullet with its name on it; the first time you Strike the target with the pistol before the end of your next turn, roll the attack roll twice and take the highest result (this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect).
36–37The pistol casts [_cloak of colors_](/Spells.aspx?ID=41) on you with a duration of 1d10 rounds.
38–47The pistol casts [_hydraulic push_](/Spells.aspx?ID=154) (4th level) as water streams from the pistol.
48–50The pistol casts [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) on you, teleporting you to the space adjacent to the target opposite your current position; if that space isn't clear, you instead teleport to the nearest open space.
51The pistol casts [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=76).
52Dozens of tattered, nonmagical playing cards burst from the pistol's barrel.
53–54Shadows crowd around the target, making all creatures [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to the target while not in bright light; this effect lasts for 1 hour.
55–57Normal vegetation within 30 feet of the target immediately dies and turns to ash.
58The pistol casts [_dinosaur form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=72) on you to transform you into a triceratops; the pistol protrudes from the base of your front horn, and you can fire (but not reload) the pistol while in this form.
59All non-artifact ammunition in your possession crumbles to dust.
60–69You are [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1 minute. You can use the extra action only to reload or fire the pistol.
70–71The target is quickened for 1 minute. It can use the extra action only to Step or Stride.
72-79The pistol casts [_vision of death_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742).
80–81Dozens of bullet holes appear in the target's nonmagical clothing. The effect is purely cosmetic.
82–85The pistol casts [_outcast's curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=211).
86The pistol vanishes, reappearing among your possessions once it can be activated again.
87–91The pistol casts [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606), but with swirling dust and sand instead of fog.
92–96The pistol casts [_mountain resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1610) on you, making your skin rough and leathery; the target ignores the resistance you gain from this spell, and the target's attacks don't reduce the spell's duration.
97–99The pistol casts [_uncontrollable dance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=347) with a duration of 3 rounds, even on a failure or critical failure.
100Reroll two results and apply both in the order rolled; further results of 100 on these rerolls have no effect.
name: Pistol of Wonder

---

The components of this ammunition emit a strong smell when combined during its activation. A creature hit by an activated aromatic ammunition gains a distinct odor for up to 1 hour or until the scent is washed off (requiring at least a gallon of water and 10 minutes of scrubbing). Any creatures within 30 feet smell the target, allowing even those with a weak sense of smell to detect its presence, and all creatures gain a +1 item bonus to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) the affected creature for as long as it has the odor. A creature that has imprecise or precise [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) doubles the range at which they can detect the target using their scent compared to the normal range of their scent.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Awakened Metal Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1189)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 168

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** round

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These bullets are formed from a liquefied high-grade precious metal and enchanted to unlock that metal's true potential. Each version has a different special effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Awakened Metal Shot (Awakened Adamantine Shot)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 168

**Price** 3,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shot is a high-grade [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271) bullet. The awakened adamantine breaks through any resistance. The shot ignores the first 20 resistance a creature has to physical damage, all damage, or piercing or bludgeoning damage. Against a [golem](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=57) or creature who already has a resistance bypassed by adamantine, or a weakness to adamantine, the shot shatters the target's defenses, causing them to become [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) for 1 minute instead. Because adamantine is uncommon, this version of _awakened metal shot_ is uncommon even for gunslingers or other characters with access to uncommon guns and bullets from this book.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Awakened Metal Shot (Awakened Cold Iron Shot)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 168

**Price** 2,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shot is a high-grade [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) bullet. The awakened cold iron attempts to disrupt [enchantments](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) the target placed on others' minds. You can name a creature you believe to be enchanted by your target or allow the shot to choose an enchanted creature randomly. On a hit, the shot attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the enchantment the target is using to manipulate that creature's mind. The counteract level is 9, and the counteract modifier is +27. If you fail (but don't critically fail) the counteract check against a [demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28) or [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), you get a success instead. If you hit a demon or fey with no active enchantment effects on other creatures, the target is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 minute instead.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Awakened Metal Shot (Awakened Silver Shot)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 168

**Price** 2,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shot is a high-grade [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=277) bullet. The awakened silver gleams with altered celestial moonlight designed to weaken and expose [devils](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=30) and [werecreatures](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=100). On a hit, it attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) effect on the target. The counteract level is 9, and the counteract modifier is +27. If you fail (but don't critically fail) the counteract check against a devil or a werecreature, you get a success instead. If you counteracted the [Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8) ability, the creature can't use that ability again for 1 round, or 1 minute on a critical success. If you hit a devil or werecreature not under a polymorph effect, the target is [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) for 1 minute instead.
name: Awakened Metal Shot

    level="1"
    right="Item 17+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Awakened Metal Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1189)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 168

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** round

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These bullets are formed from a liquefied high-grade precious metal and enchanted to unlock that metal's true potential. Each version has a different special effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Awakened Metal Shot (Awakened Adamantine Shot)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 168

**Price** 3,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shot is a high-grade [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271) bullet. The awakened adamantine breaks through any resistance. The shot ignores the first 20 resistance a creature has to physical damage, all damage, or piercing or bludgeoning damage. Against a [golem](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=57) or creature who already has a resistance bypassed by adamantine, or a weakness to adamantine, the shot shatters the target's defenses, causing them to become [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) for 1 minute instead. Because adamantine is uncommon, this version of _awakened metal shot_ is uncommon even for gunslingers or other characters with access to uncommon guns and bullets from this book.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Awakened Metal Shot (Awakened Cold Iron Shot)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 168

**Price** 2,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shot is a high-grade [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) bullet. The awakened cold iron attempts to disrupt [enchantments](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) the target placed on others' minds. You can name a creature you believe to be enchanted by your target or allow the shot to choose an enchanted creature randomly. On a hit, the shot attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the enchantment the target is using to manipulate that creature's mind. The counteract level is 9, and the counteract modifier is +27. If you fail (but don't critically fail) the counteract check against a [demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28) or [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), you get a success instead. If you hit a demon or fey with no active enchantment effects on other creatures, the target is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 minute instead.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Awakened Metal Shot (Awakened Silver Shot)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 168

**Price** 2,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shot is a high-grade [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=277) bullet. The awakened silver gleams with altered celestial moonlight designed to weaken and expose [devils](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=30) and [werecreatures](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=100). On a hit, it attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) effect on the target. The counteract level is 9, and the counteract modifier is +27. If you fail (but don't critically fail) the counteract check against a devil or a werecreature, you get a success instead. If you counteracted the [Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8) ability, the creature can't use that ability again for 1 round, or 1 minute on a critical success. If you hit a devil or werecreature not under a polymorph effect, the target is [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) for 1 minute instead.
name: Awakened Metal Shot (Awakened Adamantine Shot)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Black Powder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1190)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 169

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** round

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Black powder is a volatile and explosive alchemical substance commonly used in the production of firearm munitions. Black powder becomes inert and useless when wet and must be kept in a sealed, water-tight container.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Black Powder (Dose or Round)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 169

**Price** 1 cp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The smallest unit of black powder that still has a simple use, a dose can be a simple package paper parcel around black powder or it can be packaged with a metal bullet or pellet to be used as ammunition. When ignited with a fuse or exposed to direct flame, a dose of black powder explodes. This isn't powerful enough to deal damage but makes a loud sound and could trigger further explosions. A fuse for a dose can be created with a few twists of paper and causes the dose to explode the round after it's lit.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Black Powder (Horn)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 169

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A horn contains 500 doses worth of black powder. A horn can be detonated by leaving a small trail of black powder and then lighting it. Each 5-foot square requires one dose of black powder to create a trail through. As long as the horn is mostly full (at least 400 doses remaining) it can be detonated to deal 1d6 fire damage in a 5-foot burst (DC 16 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). It takes 1 round per 15 feet of powder trail laid down for a horn to detonate after the trail is lit. If multiple horns detonate simultaneously, it can increase the area of the explosion, but the damage in overlapping areas doesn't increase.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Black Powder (Keg)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 169

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A keg contains 5,000 doses of black powder and can be detonated in the same way as a horn. As long as the keg is mostly full (at least 4,000 doses remaining) this deals 3d6 fire damage in a 20-foot burst (DC 20 basic Reflex save). Detonating multiple kegs can increase the area, but not the damage, of this effect; detonating a keg and any horns at the same time in an overlapping area also doesn't increase the damage.
name: Black Powder

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Black Powder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1190)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 169

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** round

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Black powder is a volatile and explosive alchemical substance commonly used in the production of firearm munitions. Black powder becomes inert and useless when wet and must be kept in a sealed, water-tight container.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Black Powder (Dose or Round)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 169

**Price** 1 cp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The smallest unit of black powder that still has a simple use, a dose can be a simple package paper parcel around black powder or it can be packaged with a metal bullet or pellet to be used as ammunition. When ignited with a fuse or exposed to direct flame, a dose of black powder explodes. This isn't powerful enough to deal damage but makes a loud sound and could trigger further explosions. A fuse for a dose can be created with a few twists of paper and causes the dose to explode the round after it's lit.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Black Powder (Horn)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 169

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A horn contains 500 doses worth of black powder. A horn can be detonated by leaving a small trail of black powder and then lighting it. Each 5-foot square requires one dose of black powder to create a trail through. As long as the horn is mostly full (at least 400 doses remaining) it can be detonated to deal 1d6 fire damage in a 5-foot burst (DC 16 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). It takes 1 round per 15 feet of powder trail laid down for a horn to detonate after the trail is lit. If multiple horns detonate simultaneously, it can increase the area of the explosion, but the damage in overlapping areas doesn't increase.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Black Powder (Keg)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 169

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A keg contains 5,000 doses of black powder and can be detonated in the same way as a horn. As long as the keg is mostly full (at least 4,000 doses remaining) this deals 3d6 fire damage in a 20-foot burst (DC 20 basic Reflex save). Detonating multiple kegs can increase the area, but not the damage, of this effect; detonating a keg and any horns at the same time in an overlapping area also doesn't increase the damage.
name: Black Powder (Horn)

markdown:
**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 169 **Price** 900 gp **Ammunition** round **Activate**Interact --- The night sky seems contained in this glass projectile. When an activated _dreaming round_ damages a creature, it induces drowsiness. The creature must attempt a DC 30 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Success** The creature becomes [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) and [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature becomes fatigued and slowed 1, and must attempt another Fortitude save at the end of each of its turns. If it fails, its slowed condition increases by 1. A successful save reduces the slowed condition by 1. If the slowed condition is removed or reduced to 0, the effect and the fatigued condition end. If the target's actions are reduced to 0 by this effect, it immediately falls into a deep sleep and is [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), during which it no longer attempts a save at the end of its turn. It wakes up automatically after 1 hour or if it takes damage, but not due to non-painful stimuli (such as noise or being nudged). When it wakes up, its slowed condition decreases by 1, though it must once again save at the end of each of its turns and might risk falling asleep again.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the target is initially slowed 2.
name: Dreaming Round

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eroding Bullet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1192)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=3" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 169

**Price** 22 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** round

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Eroding bullets cast a faint green glow, and smell like the sicklysweet organic gases that rise from corpses. Handling an eroding bullet without gloves deals 1 point of acid damage and leaves the putrid scent coated on your fingers. Upon Striking an enemy,  the glass casing inside the bullet bursts, releasing a splattering of bubbling green acid that coats the target. The target takes 2d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) in addition to the damage normally dealt by the attack.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Eroding Bullet

This ammunition includes a small reservoir of a tacky red substance that coats the ammunition when you activate it. The substance makes a creature bleed more freely. For 1 minute after you deal damage to a creature with an activated exsanguinating ammunition that creature gains the listed weakness to [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). In addition, the DC of any flat checks to end persistent bleed damage increases from 15 to 17 (from 10 to 12 when receiving particularly effective assistance) for the duration.

[Fairy Bullet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1194)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=69" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 170

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The glitterdust spell cast by the fairy bullet has a DC of 23 (as is standard for a 7th-level item)._

**Price** 65 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** round

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row><actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These bullets glimmer with emerald green light that dances across the surface of the bullet like a mischievous sprite. On a successful Strike, a _fairy bullet_ casts [_revealing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653) (DC 23) extending outward from a corner of the target's space. You choose which corner of the target's space you want the burst to extend out from at the time you declare the associated Strike. Since the _fairy bullet_ is fired before _revealing light_ can reveal the target, the effects don't affect the flat check for the attack roll with the fairy bullet if the target is [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) from you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fairy Bullet

    pfs="Standard"

[Glue Bullet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1195)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

</row>

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These cartridges are filled with sticky clear glue. When a glue bullet hits, a syrupy webbing coats the target and sticks to the ground or a nearby surface, hindering their movement. The target takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 2d4 rounds, or until it [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) against a DC of 18. On a critical hit, the target is also [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) until it Escapes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Glue Bullet

<traits>

</row>

</column>

---

This craggy stone ammunition is warm to the touch. When you fire an activated _meteor shot,_ it explodes into a small swarm of meteors as it reaches its target, scorching nearby creatures and littering the ground with rubble. In addition to the weapon's normal damage, the meteor shot deals fire damage and the ground in the area becomes difficult terrain.

 In addition, the Strike gains the following critical failure effect.

**Critical Failure** The weapon misfires.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stepping Stone Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1198)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 171

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** round

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row><actions string="Single Action" /> envision, Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A series of small stone discs pressed into a single round make up this ammunition. When you fire an activated _stepping stone shot_, whether you hit or miss your target, the shot creates a series of supports in a line that creatures can walk on as if solid ground. The line can ascend or descend at a 45-degree angle. The discs support any amount of weight, but don't otherwise pose any sort of obstacle; creatures and attacks can move through them if they wish. They crumble to dust if anything attempts to move or otherwise manipulate them. A creature can use a two-action activity, which has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait, to Stride up to its speed on the stones while causing them to crumble behind it. This ammunition does not work in firearms with the [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=413) trait. The maximum length of the line depends on the type of ammunition. However, the line also can't extend beyond the maximum distance for a Strike from your firearm (usually six times the firearm's range increment).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Stepping Stone Shot
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 171

**Price** 65 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The stones reach up to 100 feet, and they last up to the end of your next turn if no one collapses them.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Stepping Stone Shot (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 171

**Price** 185 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The stones reach up to 250 feet, and they last up to 1 minute if no one collapses them.
name: Stepping Stone Shot

The line can ascend or descend at a 45-degree angle. The discs support any amount of weight, but don't otherwise pose any sort of obstacle; creatures and attacks can move through them if they wish. They crumble to dust if anything attempts to move or otherwise manipulate them. A creature can use a two-action activity, which has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait, to Stride up to its speed on the stones while causing them to crumble behind it. This ammunition does not work in firearms with the [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=413) trait. The maximum length of the line depends on the type of ammunition. However, the line also can't extend beyond the maximum distance for a Strike from your firearm (usually six times the firearm's range increment).

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Trustworthy Round](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1199)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 171

**Price** 9 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row><actions string="Free Action" /> command</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ammunition was developed in Dongun Hold to minimize casualties to friendly fire, and is always marked by a burnished copper head or tip so it can be easily identified. Before you can fire a _trustworthy round_, you must call out a target. You don't need to specify a name; the target could be “The angry tiger attacking our group on the left.” The round will only hit the specified target and will turn to gossamer dust midair if it misses the intended target or comes into contact with anything else; this also prevents abilities that redirect attacks. The round doesn't have any capabilities beyond your own to determine whether someone is who you think they are, so you can't use it to determine a disguised creature's identity. If you specify a target of “Seltyiel” and shoot someone disguised as Seltyiel who you thought was Seltyiel, the attack will still hit, whereas if you were about to hit a disguised Seltyiel who you didn't recognize to be Seltyiel, the round would dissolve. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Trustworthy Round

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sambuca](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1200)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 177

**Price** 120 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A sambuca is a long, covered troop bridge designed to help troops move from sea to land. Preparing it for use requires lashing together two [galleys](/Vehicles.aspx?ID=6) or other ships of similar size. The sambuca is then mounted across the ships on one end with its length suspended upward, and attached to pulleys fastened to the tops of the ships' masts. Once the ships are moved into position, the sambuca can be lowered to rest on a battlement or other surface, allowing troops to rush from the ships onto their intended destination.

 The sambuca has AC 17, Fort +11, Ref +5, Hardness 8, HP 32 (BT 16), and object immunities. It's covered on all sides to protect those who cross, granting them greater cover. The end of the sambuca can be lowered by teams operating the pulleys in concert. This requires three crew members on each ship, and they can lower the far end of the sambuca by 15 feet each round they work.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sambuca

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Camouflage Suit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1201)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 178

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This lightweight mesh fits easily over light armor. The suit is designed to incorporate local flora and ground clutter into the mesh to help you blend in seamlessly with the environment. Due to the abrasive nature of the materials used, this item is unsuitable for use by unarmored characters. You can prepare the suit for use within your current environment by using an exploration activity that takes at least 10 minutes, but sometimes longer if the materials are hard to find or the environment is unusual enough to warrant additional difficulty in preparing camouflage that can blend with it consistently.

 A suit prepared in this manner grants you a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks while you attempt to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) in the specific environment it has been prepared for. The suit remains usable in this manner until you rest for the night, though it doesn't grant the benefit whenever you aren't in the appropriate environment. The GM might rule that some environments are unusual enough that you can't create a camouflage suit appropriate for the environment.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

While gunslingers have many methods for practicing their aim, these sturdy paper targets are excellent for tracking a gunslinger's progress over time, keeping score of how close they came to hitting the most vital spots. These targets are also used in situations where more detailed accuracy must be recorded, such as firearm competitions. Practice targets can appear in many shapes and sizes and usually come in packs of 10 held in protective cases made of heavy cloth or light leather.
