One of the more prolifically used devices developed in the infamous Alkenstar Gunworks, these small firearm components are capable of muffling most of the weapons' explosive sound when fired. Without a silencer, a firearm's shot makes a loud and distinctive bang, which can easily be heard through doors and thin walls, but firearms equipped with silencers only make a quiet noise when fired. Due to engineering constraints, a silencer can't be attached to any firearm with the [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=413) trait. Attaching a silencer to a firearm takes 1 minute, and the silencer is consumed the first time a shot is fired through it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Silencer

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tripod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1207)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 179

**Price** 2 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Tripods are designed for use with [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=409) weapons, as a way for gunslingers with lower strength to accurately use these more powerful weapons by sacrificing mobility instead. They can be set up and attached to a firearm with a single Interact action using one hand, setting the tripod in your square. If the kickback weapon requires two hands, you can change your grip on the weapon as part of this action. While this sturdy piece of engineering is in use, you don't take the –2 penalty for firing a kickback weapon, even if your Strength isn't high enough to avoid the penalty. However, you must retrieve the tripod with a single Interact action before you can move the firearm to a different position. Normally, when you're [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39), you become observed if you do anything except Hide, [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63), or Step. However, deploying or retrieving a tripod with an Interact action doesn't automatically make you observed, so long as you don't set up or remove the tripod when it's in a spot where creatures can see the tripod itself.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tripod

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gunner's Bandolier](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1209)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 179

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This incredibly spacious bandolier can hold up to 4 one-handed crossbows or firearms that take no more than 1 action to completely reload (typically meaning that weapons with the [capacity](/Traits.aspx?ID=399) or [repeating](/Traits.aspx?ID=374) traits won't fit in the bandolier's holsters). A _gunner's bandolier_ can be etched with runes as though it were a ranged weapon. When you invest the _gunner's bandolier_, you can attune it to each of the 4 weapons holstered in it.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)    **Effect** You empower one of the attuned weapons in the bandolier, granting it the runes etched onto the _gunner's bandolier_ and removing the runes from any previously drawn weapon. Then, you Interact to draw the weapon.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)    **Effect** All weapons that were attuned to the bandolier when you invested it, not including any weapons you're currently wielding, return to the bandolier, and one of the returned weapons is automatically reloaded.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gunner's Bandolier

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Immaculate Holsters](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1210)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 180

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

**Price** 52 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This pristine leather belt is made of treated and polished black leather with silver fittings; it features a pair of matching leather holsters that can each fit a one-handed firearm or hand crossbow.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Up to two firearms currently holstered in the _immaculate holsters_ are instantly cleaned and oiled, protecting them from accidental misfires (though not misfires caused as a result of using a feat or ability). The holstered weapons are also reloaded with non-magical 0-level ammunition appropriate to a weapon of their type; if a firearm has multiple chambers, such as a slide pistol, each empty chamber is loaded. _Immaculate holsters_ can't reload the cartridge of a [repeating](/Traits.aspx?ID=374) weapon.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Immaculate Holsters

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 180 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._ **Usage** worn **Bulk** L --- This beige bandolier has a rectangular holster, which allows it to contain a standard-sized [Harrow deck](/Equipment.aspx?ID=824), and red pockets embroidered with goldwork in a strange but appealing mixture of Alkenstar and Varisian motifs. **Activate** [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You draw a card from the bandolier and Interact to load the card into a gun or crossbow you're wielding that requires 1 action to reload. The drawn card immediately transforms into magical ammunition with a type depending on the drawn card's suit, and a new copy of that card returns to the deck, ready to be drawn again. When the card transforms into ammunition, it transforms into a firearm round or crossbow bolt appropriate for the weapon you chose to reload, even if magical ammunition of the drawn variety would normally be limited to ammunition of a different type (such as a storm arrow normally only being available in arrow form). Either draw a card from the Harrow deck or roll 1d6 to determine the suit of the card. The result determines the type of magical ammunition the card becomes, as per the table below. Magical ammunition created this way lasts 10 minutes or until you fire it, whichever comes first. ## Lucky Draw Bandolier
**d6****Ability (Suit)****Ammunition**
1Strength (Hammer)[_Meteor shot_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1196)
2Dexterity (Key)[_Storm arrow_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=169)
3Constitution (Shield)[_Corrosive ammunition_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=711)
4Intelligence (Book)[_Explosive ammunition_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=162)
5Wisdom (Star)[_Terrifying ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=725)
6Charisma (Crown)[_Fairy bullet_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1194)
 Lucky Draw Bandolier **Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 180 **Price** 700 gp **Bulk** L Lucky Draw Bandolier (Greater) **Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 180 **Price** 2,000 gp **Bulk** L --- You can activate a _greater lucky draw bandolier_ once per hour instead of being able to activate it once per day.
name: Lucky Draw Bandolier

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Darkvision Scope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1212)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 180

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm or crossbow (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These scopes, popular with snipers and other sneaky sharpshooters who ply their trade in the dead of night, incorporate clouded crystals with magical properties into their design. While relatively useless under normal lighting conditions, these crystals can help bring things into focus when used in dim light. The scope is then given an enchantment to enhance these properties for use in darkness. The scope grants you a +1 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight in areas of dim light visible through the scope (as well as in areas of darkness, if the scope has been activated).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)    **Effect** You gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) until the beginning of your next turn, as long as you continue to look through the scope.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magnetite Scope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1213)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 181

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

More of a field projector than a scope, this small cylinder of magnetite has been enchanted to spread and accelerate the shrapnel of a [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=413) weapon further than the weapon's natural capabilities. Furthermore, some of the floating magnetite inside the scope's structure can help you when attempting to determine the your allies' locations. The magnetite scope grants you a +2 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=64) when using the scope to assist you in navigating. This scope can only be attached to firearms with the scatter trait.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)    **Effect** The next Strike you make this round with the gun to which the scope is attached has its scatter radius increased by 5 feet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

</traits>

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3, and activating the scope increases the scatter radius by 10 feet.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magnifying Scope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1214)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 181

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm or crossbow (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These scopes use magically enhanced lenses to extend the range of your weapon and help spot distant foes. The scope grants you a +1 item bonus to visual Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) creatures through the scope.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)    **Effect** While gazing through the scope, you zoom in on your targets to make it easier to hit them at a distance. You increase the [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) of the weapon to which the scope is attached by 5 feet until the beginning of your next turn or until you're no longer wielding the weapon, whichever comes first.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scope of Limning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1215)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 182

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The dwarven gunsmiths of Dongun Hold originally created these scopes to help them clear out vermin in underground areas. This scope captures the sound that echoes off a creature hit by the firearm and transforms it into light, illuminating the target for all to see.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** If your next Strike from the weapon to which the scope is attached hits a creature, the sound of the impact transforms into light, causing the creature to glow until the end of your next turn. A visible creature can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) while they glow. If a creature is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), they're concealed while glowing, rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39). Because the effect requires a solid impact, [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures are unaffected unless the bullet can deal force damage or has the effects of the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Scope of Limning

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 182

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons. Replace the word "transmutation" in the scope of truth with the word "polymorph."_

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm or crossbow (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _scope of truth_ is a bit bigger than most to accommodate larger lenses, which have been magically prepared with tiny shards from crystal balls to reveal the truth. The scope grants you a +2 item bonus to Perception checks made to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) in areas you can see through the scope.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For the next 10 minutes, you can see things through the scope as they actually are. The GM rolls a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check with a +20 counteract modifier and a counteract level of 7 against any [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) or [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157) in the area, but only for the purpose of determining whether you see through it, not to end the spell or effect. For instance, if the check succeeds against a [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) spell, you can see the creature's true form, but you don't end the spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Scope of Truth

    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Gunner's Saddle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1217)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 182

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn saddle

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Developed by marauders from the Mana Wastes, this clockwork saddle comes with a retractable weapon mount that can be used as a [tripod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1207) to stabilize a weapon with the [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=409) trait. Just like a normal tripod, you Interact to deploy the tripod to stabilize the firearm, and then again to retract the tripod to move it. The saddle uses complex hydraulics to protect the steed from the firearm's recoil.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gunner's Saddle

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Immovable Tripod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1218)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 182

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This copper [tripod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1207) has a magical core that can't be moved once magically locked in place—a perfect example of engineering ingenuity applied to magic items. This eccentric magical power is put to a pragmatic use of stabilizing weapons without the need for solid ground on which to stabilize them. In addition to extraplanar and aquatic environments, these devices have seen use by flying and climbing snipers who would otherwise have no way to mitigate their weapons' fierce kickback. 

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)    **Effect** You deploy the tripod and press a button to lock it into place, allowing you to deploy the tripod in midair, underwater, or anywhere else where you don't have a solid horizontal surface available. If you Activate the tripod by pushing the button again, you release and retrieve the tripod. While anchored, the tripod can be moved only if 8,000 pounds of pressure are applied to it or if a creature uses Athletics to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) the tripod with a DC of 40 (though most intelligent creatures can just push the button to release the tripod).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Immovable Tripod

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Portable Weapon Mount](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1219)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 182

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Powerful firearms like the [arquebus](/Weapons.aspx?ID=195) were originally used to defend fortifications or ships, mounted on casements or pintles to steady their aim and offset their recoil. More mobile means of stabilizing weapons with [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=409) were developed as firearms began to spread across the Inner Sea. The standard [tripod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1207) takes an Interact action to deploy using one hand.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Portable Weapon Mount (Monopod)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 182

**Price** 2 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

Monopods are lighter and can be deployed with a single hand using the same action as drawing the firearm. They still require an Interact action to retrieve. Monopods are less stable than a tripod, and firing a kickback weapon from a monopod without the necessary Strength reduces the penalty to a –1 circumstance penalty instead of removing it entirely.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Portable Weapon Mount (Tripod, Shielded)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 182

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 5

</row>

</column>

---

A shielded tripod resembles a squat shield on a tripod. You can deploy and retrieve a shielded tripod with an Interact action, as normal, but while a shielded tripod is in your square, you can use the [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=90) action to gain standard cover behind the tripod's shield. You can't use this cover to Hide or Sneak, as normal for times when your cover still leaves your position obvious.
name: Portable Weapon Mount

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Air Cartridge Firing System](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1220)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 183

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons. Weapons that install an air cartridge firing system and that have the kickback trait retain the trait and the associated drawbacks._

**Price** 75 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm (firing mechanism)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Air cartridge firing mechanisms use a container of compressed air affixed to a sealed system that releases the air in a small burst to propel ammunition, and they're the default used in Arcadian [air repeaters](/Weapons.aspx?ID=188). Any firearm can be modified to replace its normal firing mechanism with an air cartridge firing system, allowing the weapon to be fired underwater or in other conditions that would normally prevent the ignition of black powder. The air cartridges lack much of the propulsive power of black powder, however, imposing a –10-foot penalty to the attached firearm's range increment. Weapons with the [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=409) trait don't gain that trait's benefits when using an air cartridge firing system. Attaching an air cartridge firing system takes one hour, though this time can overlap with the standard time required to maintain and clean your firearm to prevent misfires.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Air Cartridge Firing System

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blazons of Shared Power](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1221)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 183

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn and attached to two weapons (see below)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These brass emblems come in a variety of designs, usually customized to the purchaser to reflect the heraldry of a family or guild. _Blazons of shared power_ come in sets of three. When you invest the blazons, you wear one of the three on your chest, and you attach the others to a pair of one-handed weapons, choosing one as the primary weapon and one as the secondary weapon. These weapons can be either melee weapons or ranged weapons. As long as you're wielding both the primary weapon and the secondary weapon, the secondary weapon gains the benefit of the fundamental runes on the primary weapon. A weapon can only have a single blazon of shared power attached to it at a time.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blazons of Shared Power
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 183

**Price** 52 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blazons of Shared Power (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 183

**Price** 1,350 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A set of _greater blazons of shared power_ also replicates property runes from the primary weapon, so long as the secondary weapon meets all the prerequisites for a given rune and isn't a specific weapon. The secondary weapon gains the benefits of those runes. All of its own runes are suppressed. When you invest the blazons, you can elect for them to transfer only fundamental runes, in which case they function as standard _blazons of shared power_.
name: Blazons of Shared Power

pfs="Standard"
>
Blazons of Shared Power (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 183

**Price** 1,350 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A set of _greater blazons of shared power_ also replicates property runes from the primary weapon, so long as the secondary weapon meets all the prerequisites for a given rune and isn't a specific weapon. The secondary weapon gains the benefits of those runes. All of its own runes are suppressed. When you invest the blazons, you can elect for them to transfer only fundamental runes, in which case they function as standard _blazons of shared power_.
name: Blazons of Shared Power (Greater)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Breech Ejectors](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1222)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 183

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm (firing mechanism)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These spring-loaded inserts can be fitted into the breech of a [double-barreled](/Traits.aspx?ID=403) firearm over the course of 10 minutes or during the firearm's daily maintenance. After the weapon is fired, the ejectors hasten the reloading process by ejecting the spent detritus from the fired rounds. This allows you to reload both barrels of the double-barreled weapon as a single Interact action the next time you reload the weapon as long as you do so before the end of your next turn. However, the ejectors are consumed in the process, and you must spend the time to insert a new set to gain the benefit again.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Breech Ejectors

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Large Bore Modifications](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1223)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 183

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

**Price** 120 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These modifications include a heavier stock and larger replacement barrel designed to increase the stopping power of firearms. Large bore modifications can only be applied to firearms with the [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=409) or [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=413) traits, and attaching large bore modifications takes 1 hour, though this time can overlap with the standard time required to maintain and clean your firearm to prevent misfires.

 When you attach large bore modifications to a kickback weapon, the bonus to damage granted by the kickback trait increases from 1 to 2 additional damage and the Strength modifier requirement to fire the weapon without penalty increases to +4.

 When you attach large bore modifications to a weapon with the scatter trait, the radius of the scatter effect increases by 5 feet, but the weapon imposes a –2 penalty on attack rolls if the wielder's Strength modifier is less than +2. If the weapon has both the kickback and scatter traits, apply both sets of modifications with the Strength modifier requirement raised to +4 to avoid penalties when firing.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Large Bore Modifications

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Underwater Firing Mechanism](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1224)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 184

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm (firing mechanism)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This device replaces the attached firearm's normal firing mechanism (normally, most of the guns in this chapter use a flintlock or matchlock firing mechanism). When the firearm's wielder fires the weapon, a small rune etched on a piece of stone affixed inside the mechanism releases a magical spark that's propelled through the firing mechanism and into the firearm, launching its bullet. An _underwater firing mechanism_ allows the attached firearm to be fired underwater or in other conditions that would normally prevent the ignition of black powder. Attaching an _underwater firing mechanism_ to a firearm takes 1 hour, though this time can overlap with the standard time required to maintain and clean your firearm to prevent misfires.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Underwater Firing Mechanism

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Adaptive Cogwheel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1225)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 184

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a firearm

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>[concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You're wielding the affixed firearm.
</row>

</column>

---

This tiny copper gear is attached to the side of a firearm with a matching bolt or pin. When you activate it, the affixed weapon magically transfigures itself into the form of any simple or martial firearm to which you have access, harmlessly ejecting any contained ammunition in the process. Any runes or attached items present on the affixed weapon remain active unless incompatible with its new form, in which case they're suppressed for the duration of the transformation. The effect lasts until the beginning of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Adaptive Cogwheel

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 184

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a firearm

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>[concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You're trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7).
</row>

</column>

---

This wide strip of treated lizard hide is wrapped around the grip or stock of the affixed weapon, augmenting the unease that your gunshot creates. When you activate it, you fire your gun into the air with the effects of [Warning Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=3164). If you already have the Warning Shot feat, the target doesn't become temporarily immune to your [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53), potentially allowing you to Demoralize them again.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Admonishing Band

</row>

---

This simple brass shell casing contains trace amounts of alchemical ingredients and is usually attached to the underside of the affixed weapon's barrel. When activated, it causes the ammunition fired from the affixed weapon to transform into your choice of acid, cold, electricity, or fire, dealing damage of the appropriate energy type instead of its usual damage as well as 1d6 [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) of the same type on a critical hit.

    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ghostshot Wrapping](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1228)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 184

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a ranged weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>[concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You attempt a ranged Strike with the affixed weapon while [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39).

 
</row>

</column>

---

This long strip of linen is tightly wound around the barrel of the affixed firearm or the grip of a bow. When activated, the talisman's magic dampens the sound of the triggering shot, rendering it completely silent, and additionally skews the angle of the shot, so it appears to come from a different location and direction than your actual position. You don't become automatically observed to any creatures due to making the triggering Strike.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ghostshot Wrapping

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>[concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You're critically hit by a firearm attack.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This talisman usually takes the form of a small sentimental object carried in a pocket or attached to the inside of a piece of armor. When you activate it, it slows the attack, and you reduce the damage from the triggering critical hit by 10, as the attack destroys the talisman. This effect only reduces the additional damage from a critical hit; it can't reduce the damage below the amount it would deal on a normal hit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Indomitable Keepsake](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1230)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 186

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>[concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You're critically hit by a firearm attack.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This talisman usually takes the form of a small sentimental object carried in a pocket or attached to the inside of a piece of armor. When you activate it, it slows the attack, and you reduce the damage from the triggering critical hit by 10, as the attack destroys the talisman. This effect only reduces the additional damage from a critical hit; it can't reduce the damage below the amount it would deal on a normal hit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Peacemaker](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1231)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 186

**Price** 35 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) </row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** Your last action was an Interact action to stow the affixed firearm or crossbow.
</row>

</column>

---

This ragged piece of white cloth is wrapped around the grip, stock, or haft of the affixed weapon. When you activate the talisman, you gain the effects of a [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=266) spell (DC 20) lasting for 1 minute. If you draw the affixed firearm, the effect ends immediately and the talisman crumbles.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Peacemaker
requirement_markdown: Your last action was an Interact action to stow the affixed firearm or crossbow.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Persistent Lodestone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1232)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 186

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a firearm with a  reload  of 1

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>[concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You miss on a ranged Strike with the affixed weapon using an ordinary 0-level piece of ammunition.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small magnetite block is attached to the barrel of the firearm by a thin metal wire drilled through a hole in its center. When you activate the lodestone, the ammunition from your missed shot is immediately recalled to your firearm, allowing you to fire again without reloading.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Persistent Lodestone

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shrieking Skull](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1233)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 186

**Price** 400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a firearm or crossbow

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>[concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You're an expert in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) and the affixed weapon is loaded.
</row>

</column>

---

This dried skull of a snake is mounted atop the firearm's barrel or affixed to a crossbow's stock. When activated, the skull crawls onto the ammunition loaded in the affixed weapon. If you Strike with the weapon before the end of your turn, the skull lets out a bloodcurdling scream as the ammunition approaches its target. Regardless of whether the Strike is a success, the screaming skull allows you to attempt to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) the target as well as each enemy within 30 feet of the target.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shrieking Skull
requirement_markdown: You're an expert in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) and the affixed weapon is loaded.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sniper's Bead](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1234)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 186

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a two-handed firearm or crossbow

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>[concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You attempt a ranged Strike with the affixed weapon before rolling

**Requirement** You're a master with the affixed weapon.
</row>

</column>

---

This plain wooden bead dangles from a string attached to the stock of the affixed weapon. When you activate the bead, its magic greatly lessens the effect of distance on your triggering attack.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sniper's Bead
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 186

**Price** 16 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You take no [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) penalty on your attack, as long as the attack is against a target within the affixed weapon's first two range increments.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sniper's Bead (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 186

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You take no range increment penalty on your attack, as long as the attack is against a target within the affixed weapon's first four range increments.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sniper's Bead (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 186

**Price** 1,600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You take no range increment penalty on your attack, even if the target is all the way out to the weapon's sixth range increment. As normal, you still can't hit a target more than six range increments away.
name: Sniper's Bead
requirement_markdown: You're a master with the affixed weapon.

This tiny, silver facsimile of a weapon [tripod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1207) is usually attached to the underside of the affixed weapon's barrel.     level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mechanical Torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1236)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 88

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Powered by electricity, you can turn a mechanical torch on and off by toggling a lever on the torch with an Interact action. When active, the torch sheds bright light in either a 20-foot radius (and dim light to the next 40 feet) or a 40-foot cone (and dim light to the next 40 feet). Changing this area requires a single Interact action to flip a switch. The torch carries sufficient charge to operate for ten minutes. You can recharge the torch in 1 minute via an integrated crank-charging mechanism, turning the clockwork gears and generating sparks to power the torch, though doing so requires two hands.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mechanical Torch

<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

**Price** 450 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This lockpick houses a small compartment containing many smaller picks and other miniature tools. The lockpick itself is surprisingly malleable, belying the components within. An almost-impossible number of other tools are hidden in the compartment, including an entire set of [infiltrator thieves' tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58) and any replacement picks, an [elite disguise kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19) and any replacement cosmetics, and an [extreme climber's kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=14), all while still somehow remaining only light Bulk. This makes the _encompassing lockpick_ a favorite discreet option for rogues infiltrating high society events, as formal wear generally has few pockets and only allows a character to wear a single tool kit of light Bulk.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Encompassing Lockpick

This brown leather satchel is made from a heavily oiled and rustic leather. The satchel is large enough to hold up to 1 Bulk worth of items.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (envision, Interact);     **Effect** Documents inside the haversack become magically disguised as documents of a similar type but with misleading information. Anyone inspecting a document notices the disguise with a successful DC 20 Perception check, but determining the actual text requires a successful DC 20 [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) check to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22). This lasts until you use this activation again, which ends the effect for all previously disguised documents; until you use the second activation to end the effect for a single document; or until a document in the haversack remains outside the haversack for at least 1 hour, ending the effect for that document.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** You revert one of the documents back to its original state.

    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scarab Cuirass](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1243)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 10

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Due to their connection to the shop’s proprietor, all Pathfinder Society agents have access to the items from Barghest’s Bin_

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The cuirass of this _+1 [deathless](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1238) [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)_, stitched to resemble an Osiriani scarab beetle, feels strangely chitinous. You gain resistance 5 to [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;     **Effect** You cast a 1st-level [_pest form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=217). You can only become a scarab beetle.

**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of _pest form_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Scarab Cuirass

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Silencing Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1244)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 10

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Due to their connection to the shop’s proprietor, all Pathfinder Society agents have access to the items from Barghest’s Bin_

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** arrow, bolt

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Silencing ammunition_ is particularly dense and seems to dampen sounds around it. On a successful Strike, an activated piece of _silencing ammunition_ creates a 2nd-level [_silence_](/Spells.aspx?ID=287) effect on the target. The target can resist the effects of the ammunition with a successful DC 25 Will save, though it takes a –2 circumstance penalty if the Strike was a critical hit. While the effect is active, the target can use an Interact action to try to tug out the arrow or bolt and end the effect early; this requires a successful DC 25 Will save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Silencing Ammunition

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Silkspinner's Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1245)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 11

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Due to their connection to the shop’s proprietor, all Pathfinder Society agents have access to the items from Barghest’s Bin_

**Price** 6,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Buckler](/Shields.aspx?ID=1)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This oddly fuzzy [buckler](/Shields.aspx?ID=1) (Hardness 10, HP 60, BT 30) resembles the abdomen of a spider. Its defensive side is covered in fine hairs, and two small spinnerets protrude from the bottom of its rim. The spinnerets serve as _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=42)_.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You animate the _silkspinner's shield_. Eight spider legs sprout from the shield's rim, granting you a climb Speed of 25 feet until the end of your next turn. Additionally, the spinnerets animate and produce silk for 1 minute. Successful shield bash Strikes made with the _silkspinner's shield_ during this time entangle the target in silk. The target takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 round. If your Strike was a critical hit, the target becomes [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) for 1 round instead. Regardless, the target can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 30) to end the effect early.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Silkspinner's Shield

markdown:
**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 11 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Due to their connection to the shop’s proprietor, all Pathfinder Society agents have access to the items from Barghest’s Bin_ **Price** 325 gp **Ammunition** arrow, bolt **Activate**Interact --- Each piece of _stonethroat ammunition_ is tipped with an unusual yellow stone. When an activated piece of _stonethroat ammunition_ hits a target, the target must attempt a DC 30 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The ammunition lodges itself loosely into the target's throat. For 1 round, the target takes a –2 status penalty to attack rolls with its jaws or fangs, and to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks.
**Failure** The ammunition embeds itself deep in the target's throat. The target can't use its jaws or fangs Strike (if it has one) or the [Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35) ability until the end of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and the target is [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) for 1 round.
name: Stonethroat Ammunition

    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Spiritual Warfare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1247)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 11

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Due to their connection to the shop’s proprietor, all Pathfinder Society agents have access to the items from Barghest’s Bin_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wand juts from an ornate hilt like a short, wooden dagger blade.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_spiritual weapon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=306) of the indicated level. When you critically hit, you apply the weapon's [critical specialization effect](WeaponGroups.aspx). In addition, you can etch one of the following property runes onto the wand: [_corrosive_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=292), [_flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295), [_frost_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296), [_shock_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303), [_thundering_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=306), or their respective greater versions. The spiritual weapon's Strikes gain the effects of this rune.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _spiritual weapon_ of the appropriate level.

</column>

This wand juts from an ornate hilt like a short, wooden dagger blade.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_spiritual weapon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=306) of the indicated level. When you critically hit, you apply the weapon's [critical specialization effect](WeaponGroups.aspx). In addition, you can etch one of the following property runes onto the wand: [_corrosive_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=292), [_flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295), [_frost_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296), [_shock_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303), [_thundering_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=306), or their respective greater versions. The spiritual weapon's Strikes gain the effects of this rune.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _spiritual weapon_ of the appropriate level.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 14

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** sling bullet

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _big rock bullet_ is a sling bullet made of heavy granite, but each piece of ammunition feels much more dense than it appears. When activated, a _big rock bullet_ transforms into a giant rock just before hitting the target. The target takes 4d6 additional bludgeoning damage on a successful hit. Even on a failed attack roll (but not a critical failure), the target takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage. Creatures with the [Catch Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=7) reaction can use it against a _big rock bullet_.

</column>

markdown:
**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 14 **Price** 10 gp **Ammunition** bolt **Activate**Interact --- The head of this bolt is a vented container that smells strongly of caustic pepper. When an activated _blindpepper bolt_ hits a target, it deals no damage; instead the creature must attempt a DC 17 Fortitude save. If the Strike was a critical success, the target takes a –2 circumstance penalty to its Fortitude save. At the GM's discretion, a creature with no eyes or olfactory organs might not be affected by this item. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the end of your next turn. It can end the effect early if it spends an action, which has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait, to wipe down its eyes.
**Failure** The target is dazzled for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) until the end of your next turn and dazzled for 1 minute.
name: Blindpepper Bolt

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 14 **Price** 6 gp --- This snare is made of magnetized weights and heavy ropes rigged to a trip wire or pressure plate. When a creature enters the square, the magnets and ropes deploy, weighing down the creature's weapons and limbs. The creature must attempt a DC 18 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes a –1 status penalty to attack rolls for 1 round or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 18).
**Failure** The creature takes a –2 status penalty to attack rolls for 1 minute or until it Escapes (DC 18).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature drops any metallic items it's holding.
name: Deadweight Snare

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Enfilading Arrow](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1253)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 14

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** arrow

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This arrow splits at the far end of the shaft into five branching arrowheads, making it impractical for single targets but deadly in combat. When you fire an activated _enfilading arrow_, it sails upward and shatters into countless copies. The arrows then rain down in a 10-foot burst at any point within your weapon's first range increment, dealing 6d8 piercing damage to all creatures in the area (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex).

 If a creature critically fails the save and you have access to the [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) critical specialization effect, the target is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) and must spend an Interact action to attempt a DC 10 Athletics check to pull the missile free; it can't move from its space until it succeeds.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Enfilading Arrow

    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Envenomed Snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1254)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
<trait label="Snare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=146" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 15

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This snare is coated with [giant wasp venom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=115) and tipped with needles, which deliver the venom to the first creature to enter the square. That creature takes 4d6 damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex) and, on a failure, the creature is exposed to the giant wasp venom.

**Craft Requirements** Supply 2 doses of giant wasp venom.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Envenomed Snare

markdown:
**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 15 **Price** 170 gp --- Fired clay covers a pit of thin mud interspersed with fragile vials of blue dragon blood. The first creature to step into the square breaks through the clay and sinks into the pit, fracturing the vials and rapidly hardening the mud as it touches the blue dragon blood. That creature must attempt a DC 29 Fortitude save as the mud hardens over its legs. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes a –5-foot circumstance penalty to its Speed for 1 minute or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 27).
**Failure** The creature is [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36), and it takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speed for 1 minute or until it Escapes (DC 27).
**Critical Failure** The creature is stunned 3, and it's [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) for 1 minute or until it Escapes (DC 27). **Craft Requirements** Supply 75 gp worth of refined [blue dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=166) blood.
name: Mudrock Snare
